perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall direct its Representatives to promptly undertake the same measures described in the preceding sentence. In the event Receiving Party elects to destroy Confidential Information, Receiving Party shall provide ORCC with written certification of such destruction executed by Receiving Party’s officer who supervised such destruction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain only such copies of Confidential Information, subject to the terms and conditions hereunder, as may be necessary or required in accordance with Receiving Party’s internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes, provided that counsel to Receiving Party may retain copies of Confidential Information of a type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1(a) hereof as it determines in good faith to be necessary to advise 2 Receiving Party in connection with any disclosure permitted by Section 4 hereof provided that all such Confidential Information shall remain subject to the provisions of this Agreement for so long as such information is retained notwithstanding any time limitations set forth in this Agreement, Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information received during the Term shall survive and continue for 1 year from and after the Effective Date; provided, however, that (i) Receiving Party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law shall survive and continue for the longer of 1 year or as long as the same remains a trade secret under applicable law and (ii) this Section 5 will not apply to any information that Receiving Party or its Representatives has previously disclosed as permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party's Representatives are not required to undertake the same measures described in the preceding sentence. The Receiving Party does not need to provide ORCC with written certification of destruction even if Receiving Party destroys Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain all copies of Confidential Information indefinitely, subject to no terms and conditions hereunder, as may be necessary or required in accordance with Receiving Party’s internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes, and counsel to Receiving Party may retain all copies of Confidential Information of any type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1(a) hereof without needing to determine it in good faith in order to advise 2 Receiving Party in connection with any disclosure permitted by Section 4 hereof and all such Confidential Information shall not be subject to the provisions of this Agreement for so long as such information is retained. Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information received during the Term shall not survive and continue for any amount of time from and after the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction within Section 5 regarding the responsibilities of the Receiving Party's Representatives, the process of destruction of confidential information, retention policies, and the survival of confidentiality obligations. The text now presents conflicting obligations regarding the protection and handling of confidential information, creating uncertainty in enforcement and raising potential legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term of Obligations: Return of Confidential Information . This Agreement shall control and govern all Confidential Information disclosed by ORCC to Receiving Party from and after the Effective Date (as defined above) for a period of one (1) year (the “Term”) unless earlier terminated by the Parties in a separate agreement or instrument executed by both Parties. Promptly upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall retain all written material (if any) containing or reflecting any Confidential Information for auditing purposes only, with no obligation to return or destroy. Receiving Party's Representatives are not required to undertake the same measures described in the preceding sentence. The Receiving Party does not need to provide ORCC with written certification of destruction even if Receiving Party destroys Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Receiving Party may retain all copies of Confidential Information indefinitely, subject to no terms and conditions hereunder, as may be necessary or required in accordance with Receiving Party’s internal records retention policies and procedures for applicable legal, compliance and regulatory purposes, and counsel to Receiving Party may retain all copies of Confidential Information of any type set forth in clause (iv) of Section 1(a) hereof without needing to determine it in good faith in order to advise 2 Receiving Party in connection with any disclosure permitted by Section 4 hereof and all such Confidential Information shall not be subject to the provisions of this Agreement for so long as such information is retained. Receiving Party’s obligations to protect Confidential Information received during the Term shall not survive and continue for any amount of time from and after the Effective Date; provided, however, that (i) Receiving Party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law shall survive and continue for the longer of 1 year or as long as the same remains a trade secret under applicable law and (ii) this Section 5 will not apply to any information that Receiving Party or its Representatives has previously disclosed as permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states obligations for Representatives and mandates written certification for destruction. The changed text explicitly states that Receiving Party's Representatives are not required to undertake the same measures and that the Receiving Party does not need to provide ORCC with written certification of destruction, conflicting with the Term of Obligations established in the contradicted text. Additionally, it states that the confidentiality obligations do not survive past the Effective Date, which goes against the initial agreement.

perturbation.original_text: ORCC owns and shall retain all right, title and interest in and to all Confidential Information, regardless of disclosure hereunder. Absolutely no present or future patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret right or other intellectual property right, nor any right, license, title or interest of any kind, is given, granted, transferred or otherwise conveyed by or under this Agreement with respect to any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall retain all right, title and interest in and to all Confidential Information, regardless of disclosure hereunder. All present and future patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret right and other intellectual property right and every right, license, title or interest of any kind, is given, granted, transferred and otherwise conveyed by or under this Agreement with respect to all Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original ownership clause in Section 6. Originally, ORCC owned all rights to the Confidential Information, and no rights were transferred to the Receiving Party. The modified version states the opposite – that the Receiving Party owns all rights and that all rights are transferred to them. This complete reversal of ownership creates a significant in-text contradiction. This contradicts Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information . Receiving Party shall retain all right, title and interest in and to all Confidential Information, regardless of disclosure hereunder. All present and future patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret right or other intellectual property right and every right, license, title or interest of any kind, is given, granted, transferred and otherwise conveyed by or under this Agreement with respect to all Confidential Information. Receiving Party shall not reverse-engineer, decompile or disassemble any software disclosed under this Agreement and shall not remove, overprint or change any notice, including as to ownership or confidentiality from any originals or copies of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the 'Receiving Party shall retain all right, title and interest in and to all Confidential Information' and that 'every right, license, title or interest of any kind, is given, granted, transferred and otherwise conveyed by or under this Agreement with respect to all Confidential Information.' This directly contradicts the original text, which stated that 'ORCC owns and shall retain all right, title and interest in and to all Confidential Information' and that no rights were transferred to the Receiving Party. The contradicted text also contains the same wording as the changed text. This creates a clear and direct in-text contradiction regarding the ownership and rights to Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall not reverse-engineer, decompile or disassemble any software disclosed under this Agreement and shall not remove, overprint or change any notice, including as to ownership or confidentiality from any originals or copies of Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party may reverse-engineer, decompile and disassemble any software disclosed under this Agreement and may remove, overprint or change any notice, including as to ownership or confidentiality from any originals or copies of Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prohibits reverse engineering and altering notices on the confidential information. The changed text permits these actions. This creates a direct in-text contradiction within Section 6, making the obligations ambiguous. This is directly contradicting Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information . Receiving Party shall retain all right, title and interest in and to all Confidential Information, regardless of disclosure hereunder. All present and future patent, copyright, trademark, trade secret right or other intellectual property right and every right, license, title or interest of any kind, is given, granted, transferred and otherwise conveyed by or under this Agreement with respect to all Confidential Information. Receiving Party may reverse-engineer, decompile and disassemble any software disclosed under this Agreement and may remove, overprint or change any notice, including as to ownership or confidentiality from any originals or copies of Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 now permits reverse engineering and altering notices, directly contradicting the original prohibition in the same section, creating a clear in-text contradiction. The 'Contradicted Text' is the same as the Changed Text, further emphasizing the direct conflict within Section 6 itself.

perturbation.original_text: For one (1) year from the Effective Date, Receiving Party (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) shall not directly or indirectly solicit, hire or engage ORCC’s employees with whom Receiving Party comes into contact, or of whom it becomes aware, in connection with this Agreement or a Transaction and whose annual base salary from ORCC exceeds $150,000, except with ORCC’s prior written consent; provided, however, that the foregoing restriction shall not apply to the hiring of ORCC’s employees who may respond to internet or other advertisements of general circulation and not specifically targeted at such employees or any action in which no Representative of Receiving Party is involved or aware of the existence of this Agreement or discussions of a possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) may directly or indirectly solicit, hire or engage ORCC’s employees with whom Receiving Party comes into contact, or of whom it becomes aware, in connection with this Agreement or a Transaction and whose annual base salary from ORCC exceeds $150,000, even without ORCC’s prior written consent; and the foregoing restriction shall apply to the hiring of ORCC’s employees who may respond to internet or other advertisements of general circulation and are specifically targeted at such employees or any action in which any Representative of Receiving Party is involved or aware of the existence of this Agreement or discussions of a possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by completely reversing the non-solicitation clause in Section 10. Originally, the Receiving Party was prohibited from soliciting or hiring certain ORCC employees without consent, with specific exceptions. The modified clause removes the prohibition and allows solicitation/hiring without consent, while also removing the exceptions. This creates an in-text contradiction on whether the Receiving Party is allowed to solicit/hire ORCC's employees, and it also affects Section 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Solicitation . Receiving Party (including its subsidiaries and affiliates) may directly or indirectly solicit, hire or engage ORCC’s employees with whom Receiving Party comes into contact, or of whom it becomes aware, in connection with this Agreement or a Transaction and whose annual base salary from ORCC exceeds $150,000, even without ORCC’s prior written consent; and the foregoing restriction shall apply to the hiring of ORCC’s employees who may respond to internet or other advertisements of general circulation and are specifically targeted at such employees or any action in which any Representative of Receiving Party is involved or aware of the existence of this Agreement or discussions of a possible Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intent and creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text. The original text prohibited solicitation, with certain exceptions, while the changed text allows solicitation without consent. The contradicted text repeats this change. This creates an in-text contradiction because the original text, before being changed, forbade solicitation and the current Changed/Contradicted text permits it. This creates a logical inconsistency because the parties' obligations are unclear. Therefore, based on the provided explanation and by the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The validity, construction and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the applicable laws of the State of Delaware without regard to that state’s principles regarding conflict of laws or choice of laws.
perturbation.changed_text: The validity, construction and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the applicable laws of the State of California with full regard to that state’s principles regarding conflict of laws or choice of laws.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates a contradiction by changing the governing law from Delaware to California. The original clause specifies Delaware law, ignoring conflict of laws principles. The modified clause switches to California law, including consideration of conflict of laws principles. This creates ambiguity about which state's laws govern the agreement and contradicts the original intent of Section 15.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Governing Law: Venue . The validity, construction and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by, and construed and enforced in accordance with, the applicable laws of the State of California with full regard to that state’s principles regarding conflict of laws or choice of laws. Each Party hereby consents, further, to the appropriate state or federal district court in Wilmington, Delaware as the proper venue and jurisdiction for the determination of any rights and the resolution of any disputes arising under or as a result of this Agreement. Nothing in this Agreement prohibits a Party from seeking specific performance, injunctive, other equitable relief, and/or damages as a remedy for a breach or threatened breach of this Agreement. The prevailing Party in any arbitration, litigation or other legal proceeding arising out of, or relating to, this Agreement shall be entitled to recover from the other Party (in addition to other relief awarded or granted) its reasonable costs and expenses, including related attorneys fees in the amount determined by the appropriate arbitrator, trial court or appellate court.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law from Delaware to California and includes consideration of conflict of laws principles, which directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text specifies California law but simultaneously designates Wilmington, Delaware as the venue and jurisdiction, creating a clear contradiction regarding the applicable law and jurisdiction. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, as different sections (the changed text and the contradicted text) now provide conflicting statements about which state's laws govern the agreement and where disputes should be resolved.
